profile.equipment: jj labs microphone, fender heartfield electric guitar, vester acoustic guitar, washburn XB-100 bass, west-african djembe and august hoffman piano
customized fender strat, ibanez acoustic guitar, yamaha bass, yamaha sy35 and polish sucky violin
profile.trivia: Micce was a key character in the history of the band and transformed the previously unfocused and complex musical vision to something more simplistic and comprehendable. This formed the new foundation of all later music produced by the band even after his departure.

The Johansson brothers joined the band during a rather unstable period as a final attempt at keeping the band as a live performing act. They did well but couldn't stop the band from transforming into a studio band, a band that didn't allow more members than Niclas and Micce.

The Johansson brothers and especially Rikard was probably the first to introduce the idea of blending elements of alternative music with a dark edge into the band like for instance portishead. This later on became another signum for the band and a way to differ from the mainstream.

With Martin as a third member came the idea of starting to play live, something that later on was abandoned for various reasons. Martin also contributed with some great music and lyrics for the mCD "where dreams turn to dust" and thus left his mark in the history of the band.

Robert became quite infamous for not showing up at rehearsals which later on ended his short career in the band. He also contributed to the less successfull and final live performance at chalmers in 2000.

Tommy had double-pedals and thus we thought he might be a great drummer. Unfortunatelly, we never saw him using these. This, his background as a drummer in a ska-band, the fact that he was really lazy and last but not least his lack of motivation limited his career to a few rehearsals.

With Johan as a second member came the first attempt at creating a real band based on the rough ideas and visions that constituted the core of forest of shadows at that stage. There was some occasional jamming but it never really resulted in anything.

Martin was (is?) a friend of Micce and a really good bassist. When we started planning our first gig he came up as a potential bass-player. Since he wasn't into metal, at all, he never wasn't intended as more than a session-member. The day before this historical first gig he broke his foot and really messed up everything. In the 11th hour Martin Petterson (ex Shubend) appeared and saved the situation.

When we lost our session bassist to a foot injury the day before our first gig was to take place we thought that all hope was lost. After having ideas of skipping the bass and just make the guitars more bassy we phoned Martin who showed up some hour later. We rehearsed unplugged for some hour and most likely Martin didn't really learn the notes. Still, the gig took place and nobody commented on the bass. Either he played ok or the rest of us played really bad.

Just having a violin to complement the sound wasn't good enough for us so we contacted Anna, a friend of Micce, and asked her if she wanted to add some flute. She was very happy about it and pretty much improvised the entire intro of "eternal autum". This intro-piece definatelly added to the magic of the mCD "where dreams turn to dust".

We really needed a drummer and since we had given up hope to find a fulltime member after the failures with Tommy we thoght we might as well go for a session drummer. Oskar, a friend of Micce was definatelly good enough and we rehearsed with him a couple of times. While this was happening Martin C was gradually leaving the band and so was our hope of remaining a live act. I guess that also resulted in that we stopped rehearsing with Oskar. It was a strange time.
